## [03:00 - 06:01]

He doesn't know what to do with himself. He's so angry. He looks to my mum for instructions. He's now crying for real. He leaves me in the lounge room, picks up my mum and they both walk into the kitchen. You probably remembered it was the kitchen. It was the kitchen. Now I'm going to ask you another question. I purchased a lot of books from a gentleman when I was trying to court his mentorship. How many books of his did I buy? How many books of his did I buy? And I spent all of my savings in 2014. I purchased a thousand of his books. You probably got that too. Wasn't that crazy? This is why storytelling is so powerful because it's sticky. It's sticky. People can remember it. They can recall it faster. I use this analogy often when I say that when you share stories, you're taking up more hard drive space in people's heads. You're taking up hard drive space. I'm taking up a lot of hard drive space in your head right now. And the more hard drive space you take up in somebody's head, the more connected they will feel to you. The more connected they're going to feel to you. Often what happens in relationships where we don't storytell, we don't feel connected to them. This is why I know so many people who when they come to my course, they say, well, Vin, I've only done your course for three days in my in-person classes. And they say to me, but I feel so connected to you. Whereas I've worked with my manager for the last eight years and I've never felt connected to them. And the reason that happens is because, well, the manager and that particular employee, they don't storytell with each other. And when you don't storytell, you're not taking up hard drive space. Therefore, you don't feel connected to the person. I'll ask you a really bold question right now and you can just answer in your head. Do you feel connected to me? Do you? And if you don't, well, then you just totally ruined my point. But if you do feel connected to me, we've probably never met in person. Think about this. You're feeling connected to someone that you're just watching on screen right now. And you feel connected to me again, because I'm taking up more hard drive space in your head. Now, the second reason why storytelling is so powerful is simply because when you storytell and you have good structure in your story, what happens is it satisfies the brain. So when you've got good structure, your brain goes, Oh, yes, thank you. Good structure. And then more than this, when you've got good characters in your story and you bring them to life, it accesses people's emotions. It accesses their heart. And when you've got a strong point to your story, it hits people in the gut. People feel it. This is why you'll hear so many storytelling teachers say that storytelling is a full body experience. Now, there are many reasons again, I can get into why storytelling is so powerful. I'll just share with you one more. And the last one is that when you share information with people and you don't use stories, this will make people think. However, when you share stories, you can actually move people not just to think, but you can move them

to act. So the last one I'll say is that storytelling is more influential. Because when you share stories, you can actually move them to take action on something. Let's get into the formula for storytelling. See you in the next video.
